reviews.author: coderabbitai[bot]
reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 5**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In
`@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py`:
- Around line 53-54: The handler currently hardcodes the trace topic in
_DELEGATION_ATTEMPT_TOPIC; instead, declare
"onex.evt.omnimarket.delegation-attempt.v1" in contract.yaml and resolve it via
the same contract-backed lookup used for _DEFAULT_DELEGATION_TOPIC (i.e., remove
_DELEGATION_ATTEMPT_TOPIC literal, add a new contract key for the
delegation-attempt topic, and replace references with the contract resolver call
used elsewhere in adapter_llm_dispatch.py so the handler obtains the topic from
the contract).
- Around line 355-360: Right now the code treats any successful json.loads(raw)
as a full quality pass by instantiating ModelQualityGateResult(ruff_pass=True,
import_pass=True, test_pass=True), which is incorrect; instead, only record that
parsing succeeded (e.g., set a parse_ok flag or a minimal parse result) and set
accepted based on parse only, and defer creating/updating ModelQualityGateResult
(or setting ruff_pass/import_pass/test_pass) until the actual gate checks run;
update the block around json.loads(raw), the variables accepted and gate, and
any downstream usage so that ModelQualityGateResult booleans are only assigned
from the real gate stage rather than parse success.
- Around line 368-409: The code currently swallows exceptions from
_call_endpoint() and returns a fallback plan, making failures look like
successful dispatches; change this so you persist the trace (using
ModelQualityGateResult, ModelDispatchTrace, _write_trace and _emit_trace_to_bus)
and then re-raise the original exception so callers (handle()) can treat it as a
failure. Concretely: in the except Exception as exc block keep building
gate/trace/raw/accepted as you do, ensure raw/accepted are set to safe values,
call _write_trace(trace, self._state_dir) and _emit_trace_to_bus(trace) and then
raise the caught exception (raise) instead of falling through to the JSON
parsing/plan return path so handle() will not increment total_dispatched for
failed endpoint calls.

In `@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_dod_verify/handlers/handler_dod_verify.py`:
- Around line 73-74: Remove the dead assignment to the instance variable by
deleting the line that sets self._started_at = started_at (it is never read);
alternatively if intended to use that timestamp, update run_verification() to
use the stored self._started_at instead of creating a new local started_at—refer
to the assignment site and the run_verification() method to apply one clear fix
(prefer simply removing the unused self._started_at assignment).
- Around line 35-39: The assignment to self._started_at in the handle() method
is unused; remove the line setting self._started_at = started_at and rely on the
local started_at used inside run_verification(), ensuring run_verification()
continues to pass its local started_at into make_completed_event(); update only
the handle() method (referenced symbol: handle) and leave run_verification() and
make_completed_event() unchanged.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>🪄 Autofix (Beta)</summary>

Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR:

- [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "4b0d0e0a-96d7-4f10-b296-3a18ea78f0b9"} --> Push a commit to this branch (recommended)
- [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "ff5b1114-7d8c-49e6-8ac1-43f82af23a33"} --> Create a new PR with the fixes

</details>

---

inline_comments.author: coderabbitai[bot]
inline_comments.body:
_🛠️ Refactor suggestion_ | _🟠 Major_

**Move the trace topic out of the handler.**

This introduces a second hardcoded bus topic in a `node_*/handlers` module. It bypasses the same contract-backed lookup pattern this file already uses for `_DEFAULT_DELEGATION_TOPIC`, so the trace event can drift from the published contract. Please declare `onex.evt.omnimarket.delegation-attempt.v1` in `contract.yaml` and resolve it from there instead of embedding the literal here.

As per coding guidelines "Never hardcode topics in handler code; declare them in contract.yaml instead".

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py`
around lines 53 - 54, The handler currently hardcodes the trace topic in
_DELEGATION_ATTEMPT_TOPIC; instead, declare
"onex.evt.omnimarket.delegation-attempt.v1" in contract.yaml and resolve it via
the same contract-backed lookup used for _DEFAULT_DELEGATION_TOPIC (i.e., remove
_DELEGATION_ATTEMPT_TOPIC literal, add a new contract key for the
delegation-attempt topic, and replace references with the contract resolver call
used elsewhere in adapter_llm_dispatch.py so the handler obtains the topic from
the contract).
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

inline_comments.author: coderabbitai[bot]
inline_comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Don't treat `json.loads()` as a passed quality gate.**

A successful parse is not the same thing as `ruff_pass`, `import_pass`, and `test_pass`. With this branch, any parseable JSON — even `[]` or `"ok"` — is recorded as a fully passing structural gate in the authoritative trace before those checks ever run. Please keep parse validation separate from `ModelQualityGateResult`, and only set these booleans from the real gate stage.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py`
around lines 355 - 360, Right now the code treats any successful json.loads(raw)
as a full quality pass by instantiating ModelQualityGateResult(ruff_pass=True,
import_pass=True, test_pass=True), which is incorrect; instead, only record that
parsing succeeded (e.g., set a parse_ok flag or a minimal parse result) and set
accepted based on parse only, and defer creating/updating ModelQualityGateResult
(or setting ruff_pass/import_pass/test_pass) until the actual gate checks run;
update the block around json.loads(raw), the variables accepted and gate, and
any downstream usage so that ModelQualityGateResult booleans are only assigned
from the real gate stage rather than parse success.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

✅ Addressed in commits ce5e587 to 8a4f849

inline_comments.author: coderabbitai[bot]
inline_comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

**Re-raise endpoint failures after persisting the trace.**

This block swallows `_call_endpoint()` exceptions, writes the failed trace, and then returns a fallback plan. `handle()` will still review/delegate that placeholder and increment `total_dispatched`, so a hard LLM outage now looks like a successful dispatch. Keep the trace write, but bubble the endpoint failure afterward (or return an explicit failure signal that `handle()` checks) so failed attempts are traced without being delegated.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py`
around lines 368 - 409, The code currently swallows exceptions from
_call_endpoint() and returns a fallback plan, making failures look like
successful dispatches; change this so you persist the trace (using
ModelQualityGateResult, ModelDispatchTrace, _write_trace and _emit_trace_to_bus)
and then re-raise the original exception so callers (handle()) can treat it as a
failure. Concretely: in the except Exception as exc block keep building
gate/trace/raw/accepted as you do, ensure raw/accepted are set to safe values,
call _write_trace(trace, self._state_dir) and _emit_trace_to_bus(trace) and then
raise the caught exception (raise) instead of falling through to the JSON
parsing/plan return path so handle() will not increment total_dispatched for
failed endpoint calls.
```

</details>

📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Adds dispatch tracing infrastructure to LLM adapter with Kafka integration for per-attempt trace records, introduces Pydantic models for trace data capture, and refactors the DOD verify handler from a two-step flow to a single canonical entry point. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Dispatch Tracing Implementation**
`src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py`, `src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/models/model_dispatch_trace.py`, `tests/unit/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/test_dispatch_trace.py`|Added tracing infrastructure to capture LLM dispatch attempts with JSON validation, quality gate results, and optional review outcomes. Traces are persisted to `dispatch-traces/` directory and conditionally emitted to Kafka. New Pydantic models `ModelQualityGateResult`, `ModelReviewResult`, and `ModelDispatchTrace` formalize trace structure. Comprehensive unit and integration tests validate trace writing, emission, and handling of both success and failure paths.| |**Handler Refactoring**
`src/omnimarket/nodes/node_dod_verify/handlers/handler_dod_verify.py`|Replaced two-step flow (`start` → `run_checks`) with single canonical `handle(command, evidence_results=None)` entry point that constructs state directly. Retained `run_verification(...)` as public wrapper. Removed prior handler shim and old entry points.| ## Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Dispatch as AdapterLlmDispatch participant LLM as LLM Model participant Validator as JSON Validator participant Gate as Quality Gate participant StateDir as State Directory participant Kafka as Kafka Broker Dispatch->>Dispatch: _generate_plan_traced(attempt=1) Dispatch->>Dispatch: Start timing Dispatch->>LLM: Call model.invoke() LLM-->>Dispatch: Raw response text Dispatch->>Validator: Validate JSON structure alt JSON Valid Validator-->>Dispatch: Parsed plan_dict Dispatch->>Gate: Evaluate quality gates Gate-->>Dispatch: ModelQualityGateResult (pass/errors) Dispatch->>Dispatch: accepted = all_pass else JSON Invalid Validator-->>Dispatch: Validation error Dispatch->>Dispatch: Wrap in fallback structure Dispatch->>Gate: Evaluate quality gates Gate-->>Dispatch: ModelQualityGateResult (failures) Dispatch->>Dispatch: accepted = False end Dispatch->>Dispatch: Build ModelDispatchTrace (with metrics, duration) Dispatch->>StateDir: Write trace JSON to dispatch-traces/{id}-attempt-{n}.json StateDir-->>Dispatch: File written alt KAFKA_ENABLED Dispatch->>Kafka: Emit trace to onex.evt.omnimarket.delegation-attempt.v1 Kafka-->>Dispatch: Message sent end Dispatch->>Dispatch: Return (plan_dict, trace) ``` ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~50 minutes ## Poem > 🐰 Traces now flutter like clover in spring, > Each LLM whisper gets captured on wing, > To Kafka they flow, their stories well-kept, > While handlers dance cleanly—refactored and swept. 📝✨
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 2 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 43.33% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (2 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :---------------: | :------- | :---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title accurately summarizes the main change: adding dispatch tracing functionality with persistent storage to .onex_state/ directory. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `jonah/omn-7855-dispatch-tracing`
